products.title: Splanky Pot Green 14 cm
products.body_html:
Cool, tactile, and completely at home in a modern space. The Splanky Pot in Green is a two-tiered reactive glaze ceramic pot, finished in a sea-glass green that shifts between slate and sage depending on the light. The tiered silhouette — a wider upper bowl sitting on a narrower base — gives it a distinctly sculptural quality that stands out on its own, even before you add a plant.

The reactive glaze process means the colour has a natural depth and variation you simply can't replicate by hand. Ideal for ferns, calatheas, or any plant that enjoys a little humidity and a lot of admiration.

Good to know: This is a decorative pot with no drainage hole. We recommend keeping your plant in its growing pot inside the Splanky Pot, and removing it to water.

- Dimensions: 14 cm (diameter) × 15 cm (height)

- Material: Reactive glazed ceramic

- Drainage hole: No

- Fits growing pot size: P13 or smaller

products.title: Otis Pot Ocher 13 cm
products.body_html:
Warm, handcrafted character in a compact package. The Otis Pot in Ocher is a glazed ceramic pot finished in a rich golden-amber glaze, topped with a delicate scalloped lace detail around the rim. No two pots fire the same way, so the depth and warmth of the colour shifts ever so slightly across the surface — which means yours is truly one of a kind.

Its rounded silhouette sits on a gently raised foot, giving it a grounded yet elegant presence on a windowsill or shelf. Pair it with a trailing or compact houseplant for a warm, lived-in feel.

Good to know: This is a decorative pot with no drainage hole. We recommend keeping your plant in its growing pot inside the Otis Pot, and removing it to water. This protects the roots and keeps your Otis looking beautiful for longer.

- Dimensions:13 cm (diameter) × 15.5 cm (height)

- Material: Glazed ceramic

-Drainage hole: No

- Fits growing pot size: P12 or smaller

products.title: Cabbage tree | Cordyline australis
products.body_html:
Cabbage Tree – Bold, Tropical-Looking, Surprisingly Hardy

Cordyline australis is one of the most useful and reliably dramatic plants you can have in a UK garden. Those long, sword-like leaves — erupting from the top of a thickening woody stem in a bold, fountain-like spray — create an instantly exotic, architectural silhouette that looks tropical but handles British weather with real tenacity. At 100 cm, this is already a genuinely impressive specimen, and it will only get bolder from here.

Plant Personality & Aesthetic Appeal

The visual impact of Cordyline australis is immediate and strong. The long, narrow, pointed leaves radiate outward from the growing crown in every direction, creating a spiky, explosive form that adds real energy and structure to any planting. The green-grey colouring is clean and bold, and the leaves catch and scatter light beautifully throughout the day. In a large border alongside tropical companions, in an oversized container flanking an entrance, or as a standalone patio specimen, Cordyline australis looks confident and deliberate — the kind of plant that anchors a scheme and makes everything around it look more intentional.

Growth Habit & Size Potential

Cordyline australis grows as a single-stemmed specimen that gradually forms a visible trunk over several years. The crown of leaves sits at the top of the developing stem, growing upward and producing an increasingly impressive canopy of foliage. At 100 cm in a 26 cm pot, this is already a characterful specimen with real presence. Mature specimens outdoors can reach 5 to 10 m in ideal conditions, though container-grown plants are considerably more manageable. After several years, established plants may produce side branches, creating a multi-headed look of even greater impact.

Light & Location Needs

Full sun or very light partial shade suits Cordyline best. It thrives in a sheltered, sunny position — a courtyard, a south-facing border, a sheltered patio — and is perfectly comfortable in a large container indoors in a bright, sunny room. It is one of the hardiest of the exotic-looking garden plants and will comfortably survive the vast majority of UK winters without any protection in milder areas. In colder regions, a wrap of horticultural fleece around the crown in hard frosts is useful insurance.

Care Level & Maintenance Tips

Cordyline australis is refreshingly easy to care for. Water regularly while establishing, then reduce — it is quite drought tolerant once settled. In containers, water moderately through the growing season and less in winter. Remove old, brown lower leaves by pulling them down and off cleanly to keep the plant looking tidy and to reveal the developing stem. Feed with a balanced fertiliser in spring. Repot container specimens every two to three years. A position sheltered from the most severe winter winds helps maintain the best leaf appearance.

Cordyline australis: Exotic Ambition, British Reliability

Part of what makes Cordyline australis such a satisfying garden plant is the disparity between how it looks and how easy it is. It looks like something you should be coddling and protecting and worrying about — and instead it just quietly thrives, growing steadily bolder and more impressive each season. On a sunny urban terrace, in a garden room, or anchoring a tropical border scheme, it brings a confidence and visual impact that takes years to develop and requires very little to maintain.

Why You'll Love the Cabbage Tree

  • Exotic Look, Hardy Nature: Creates a tropical atmosphere while handling most UK winters without complaint.
  • Excellent Container Specimen: Works brilliantly in large pots flanking doorways or anchoring patio displays.
  • Year-Round Architectural Structure: The bold, spiky form provides strong visual interest in every season.
  • Versatile Indoor and Outdoor Use: Equally at home in a bright conservatory or a sheltered garden border.
  • Low Maintenance Once Established: Drought tolerant, largely self-sufficient, and very forgiving of occasional neglect.

Bold, Beautiful, and Built for British Gardens

At 100 cm, this Cordyline australis is ready to make a statement from day one. Plant it in a sunny spot, let it establish, and watch it become one of the most reliably impressive plants in your entire garden.

What You'll Receive

  • Size at dispatch: 100 cm in height, and comes in a 26 cm pot
  • Care level: Easy
  • Pet safety: No, toxic to pets

products.title: Variegated century plant | Agave americana 'Mediopicta'
products.body_html:
Variegated Century Plant – Striking, Architectural, Desert Chic

Agave americana 'Mediopicta' is one of the most visually arresting agaves in cultivation. Long, sword-shaped leaves in deep blue-green are divided down the centre by a broad, creamy-yellow stripe that runs the full length of each leaf, creating a bold, two-tone effect that is both sculptural and genuinely beautiful. This is a plant that looks at home on sun-baked Mediterranean terraces and modern outdoor rooms alike — architectural, low-maintenance, and permanently impressive.

Plant Personality & Aesthetic Appeal

The variegation of 'Mediopicta' is the showstopper — that clean, wide central stripe of creamy yellow against the blue-green leaf margin creates a strong graphic impact that photographs beautifully and holds its interest in every season. The leaves are long and strap-like with toothed margins and a terminal spine, giving the rosette a pleasingly fierce quality. As the rosette develops, the overlapping, symmetrically arranged leaves create a perfect spiral pattern when viewed from above. It is the kind of plant that creates genuine visual excitement — structured, bold, and quite unlike anything soft and fluffy.

Growth Habit & Size Potential

Agave americana is a large-growing species and 'Mediopicta' is no exception. At 60 cm in a 26 cm pot, this is already a substantial, impressive specimen. Outdoors in warm conditions it will continue to develop into an increasingly large, dramatic rosette over the coming years. The terminal spines on the leaves are genuinely sharp — position with some consideration for passers-by. Well-suited to large containers on a sunny patio, Mediterranean-style garden beds, gravel gardens, and contemporary roof terraces.

Light & Location Needs

Full sun is essential. Agave americana 'Mediopicta' is a desert species that loves the brightest, hottest, driest conditions available. Indoors, place in front of the sunniest south-facing window. Outdoors, position in an open, south-facing spot with free-draining soil or a gritty container mix. It tolerates light frost when perfectly dry, but in the UK it is safer to protect against hard winters — bring undercover or wrap with fleece during very cold spells.

Care Level & Maintenance Tips

Water sparingly — agaves are extraordinary drought tolerant and most problems come from overwatering rather than underwatering. In summer, water when the compost is fully dry; in winter, barely water at all. Use a very free-draining compost with added grit or perlite. Feed once in spring with a low-nitrogen, high-potassium fertiliser. Handle with care — the terminal leaf spines are genuinely sharp and the leaf margins have teeth. No pruning required; old outer leaves can be removed at the base as they die off naturally.

Agave 'Mediopicta': Sculpture That Grows

There is a strong, minimal beauty to a well-grown agave that connects to the wider world of contemporary design. 'Mediopicta' takes that quality and elevates it with the boldness of its variegation — the yellow and blue-green contrast is strong enough to read from a distance and detailed enough to reward close inspection. In a large, simple pot on a sun-baked terrace, this plant brings the feeling of somewhere hot and considered and precisely designed.

Why You'll Love the Variegated Century Plant

  • Striking Two-Tone Variegation: That broad creamy-yellow stripe against blue-green is genuinely spectacular.
  • Virtually Indestructible: One of the most drought-tolerant plants in cultivation — forgets to need water.
  • Permanent Architectural Form: Creates strong, sculptural structure in any setting, all year round.
  • Low Maintenance: Give it sun, give it drainage, and largely leave it alone.
  • Perfect for Modern Gardens: Suits contemporary terraces, gravel gardens, and minimalist outdoor spaces.

Bold, Beautiful, and Built to Last

Agave americana 'Mediopicta' is the statement plant for sun-lovers who want dramatic form without demanding care. Give it the sunniest spot you have and let that extraordinary variegation do all the talking.

What You'll Receive

  • Size at dispatch: 60 cm in height, and comes in a 26 cm pot
  • Care level: Easy
  • Pet safety: No, toxic to pets

products.title: Italian cypress | Cupressus sempervirens 'Totem'
products.body_html:
Italian Cypress – Columnar, Elegant, Mediterranean Classic

Cupressus sempervirens 'Totem' is the definitive architectural evergreen for anyone seeking that clean, columnar Mediterranean silhouette. Perfectly narrow, pencil-straight columns of deep blue-green foliage reach skyward, bringing a strong, graphic verticality to garden schemes that is almost impossible to replicate with any other plant. Whether flanking a doorway, lining a path, or standing alone as a focal point, this cypress makes a considered and timeless statement.

Plant Personality & Aesthetic Appeal

'Totem' is all about the line. The habit is strictly upright and columnar — branches held closely against the central stem rather than spreading outward — producing that instantly recognisable narrow spire shape that defines the Tuscan landscape. The foliage is a deep, slightly blue-green with a fine, dense texture and a distinctive, pleasant resinous fragrance when brushed. At 110 cm, this is already a compelling specimen that reads clearly as an architectural element rather than just a garden plant. It brings a formal, European quality to any setting it occupies.

Growth Habit & Size Potential

Italian cypress is strictly columnar throughout its life — the narrow habit is its defining characteristic and it does not widen significantly as it ages. Growth is relatively slow once established, adding roughly 20 to 30 cm per year in good conditions. Mature specimens can ultimately reach 8 to 15 m tall while remaining very narrow. At 110 cm in a 23 cm pot, this is a well-established specimen that will continue to develop its striking vertical form year on year. Suits formal planting in pairs, lining paths or driveways, and roof terrace or large container planting.

Light & Location Needs

Full sun is essential for Italian cypress. It is a Mediterranean native that thrives in open, sun-baked, free-draining conditions and will not perform well in shade. Plant in the sunniest available spot — south-facing positions are ideal. It is wind-tolerant and happy in coastal locations. In the UK, mild, sheltered areas give the best results; while reasonably frost-hardy, young specimens may need some protection in very cold winters.

Care Level & Maintenance Tips

Italian cypress is genuinely low maintenance once established. Water regularly while settling in during the first season, then significantly reduce — it is highly drought tolerant and prefers dry to wet conditions. Avoid overwatering, which is the most common mistake with this plant. No pruning is required or recommended, as cutting into the narrow form can permanently damage the natural silhouette. Feed with a slow-release general fertiliser in spring. Ensure excellent drainage — waterlogged soil is the main cause of decline.

Italian Cypress: Bringing the Tuscan Hillside to Your Garden

There is something immediately evocative about a column of Italian cypress in a garden. It shifts the mood — towards warmth, towards formality, towards a sense of considered design. In pairs flanking an entrance or gate, the effect is particularly striking, creating an instant architectural statement that also happens to be very easy to maintain. It is the kind of garden design decision that makes a space look much more intentional than it might otherwise.

Why You'll Love Italian Cypress

  • Perfect Columnar Form: The narrow, pencil-straight silhouette is one of the most architecturally distinctive shapes in the plant world.
  • Evergreen Year-Round Interest: Keeps its deep green colour and clean shape in every season.
  • Very Low Maintenance: Once established, drought-tolerant and requiring no pruning or regular intervention.
  • Instantly Mediterranean: A single plant shifts the mood of a garden or terrace entirely.
  • Excellent for Formal Schemes: Perfect for flanking doorways, lining paths, and creating formal garden structure.

The Column That Does All the Work

Cupressus sempervirens 'Totem' is one of the clearest statements you can make in a garden — architectural, elegant, and entirely unfussy. Plant it in full sun, give it good drainage, and enjoy years of that extraordinary vertical presence.

What You'll Receive

  • Size at dispatch: 110 cm in height, and comes in a 23 cm pot
  • Care level: Easy
  • Pet safety: No, toxic to pets

products.title: Red Abyssinian banana | Ensete ventricosum 'Maurelii'
products.body_html:
Red Abyssinian Banana – Bold, Tropical, Unmistakably Dramatic

Ensete ventricosum 'Maurelii' is one of the boldest, most dramatically coloured foliage plants you can grow in the UK. Enormous paddle-shaped leaves emerge flushed with deep blood-red midribs and undersides, fading to a rich olive-burgundy above, creating an intensity of colour that makes most other garden plants look quite understated. This is tropical drama at its very best.

Plant Personality & Aesthetic Appeal

'Maurelii' produces huge leaves — reaching 1 to 2 m on well-grown specimens — with a striking two-toned character: deep red to maroon beneath and along the midrib, rich olive to bronze-green above. The midrib is particularly striking, running the full length of the leaf in a vivid scarlet that catches the light intensely. The overall effect is sculptural, bold, and unmistakably tropical — it reads as a statement from across the garden. Few plants bring this quality of colour combined with sheer scale. Against a white wall, alongside jungle-inspired plantings, or as a centrepiece in a large container display, 'Maurelii' is exceptional.

Growth Habit & Size Potential

Ensete is a fast-growing, upright, clump-free plant — unlike true bananas it does not produce offshoots, growing as a single, increasingly dramatic specimen. At 80 cm in a 26 cm pot, this is a substantial young plant already showing its characteristic form. In a warm, sheltered, sunny spot during the British summer it grows remarkably fast, potentially reaching 2 to 3 m in a single season in ideal conditions. It is not frost-hardy and must be brought indoors or stored over winter in the UK.

Light & Location Needs

Ensete 'Maurelii' loves warmth and sun. The most intense leaf colour and fastest growth comes from a sunny, sheltered, south-facing position. A large conservatory, a bright glass-house, or a sheltered patio in full sun are all ideal during the warmer months. It needs protection from temperatures below 5°C, so in the UK it is grown as a seasonal specimen outdoors from late spring to early autumn, then overwintered undercover.

Care Level & Maintenance Tips

Water generously during the growing season — this is a thirsty, fast-growing plant that benefits from regular, deep watering in summer. Feed every two weeks with a high-nitrogen liquid feed to encourage the fastest, most impressive leaf growth. Use a large, rich, well-drained compost. Before the first frosts, cut the leaves back and bring the plant inside to a frost-free location. It can be overwintered with minimal water in a cool but frost-free space. Protect the central growing point above all else.

Ensete 'Maurelii': Tropical Drama on a British Patio

Part of the pleasure of growing Ensete 'Maurelii' is the sheer speed and scale of it — the leaves seem to grow before your eyes in a warm British summer, and the colour never stops being surprising. Walking past that deep red midrib and those enormous paddle leaves is an experience. It transforms a patio from pleasant to genuinely spectacular, and it invites everyone who sees it to come closer and look properly.

Why You'll Love the Red Abyssinian Banana

  • Extraordinary Leaf Colour: Deep red midribs and burgundy-toned foliage unlike anything else in the garden.
  • Fast-Growing Statement Plant: Grows rapidly in a warm British summer to create immediate tropical impact.
  • Bold Container Plant: Perfect as a centrepiece in a large patio container or courtyard display.
  • Unique, Eye-Catching Specimen: Guaranteed to start conversations and earn genuine admiration.
  • Indoor-Outdoor Versatility: Grows outdoors in summer, overwinters indoors with minimal fuss.

Bring the Tropics to Your Summer Garden

Ensete ventricosum 'Maurelii' is the plant for anyone who wants their garden to look genuinely extraordinary from June to October. Give it the warmth, the water, and the sun it deserves, and it will deliver something truly spectacular.

What You'll Receive

  • Size at dispatch: 80 cm in height, and comes in a 26 cm pot
  • Care level: Medium
  • Pet safety: Non-toxic to cats and dogs. We still recommend discouraging pets from eating any plant.

products.title: Tusca Pot
products.body_html:
The Tusca Pot is a timeless ceramic planter with a clean, minimalist design that fits effortlessly into any interior. The smooth matte finish and simple shape allow the focus to remain on the plant while still adding a modern decorative touch to your space.

Made from durable powder-coated ceramic, the Tusca Pot is sturdy, long-lasting, and ideal for houseplants of all sizes. The uniform shape makes it easy to place nursery pots inside, making repotting and plant care simple.

Available in multiple colours and sizes, the Tusca Pot is perfect for shelves, desks, windowsills, and plant displays, and works well whether used alone or grouped with other sizes.

products.title: Old Zinc Pot
products.body_html:
Old Zinc Tub

The Old Zinc Tub brings a rustic, vintage look to any garden, patio, or indoor plant display. Inspired by traditional zinc tubs once used for household tasks, this oval container adds character while remaining highly practical. Its generous size makes it perfect for planting multiple plants together, creating herb gardens, seasonal arrangements, or even small water features.

The antique zinc finish gives each tub a timeless, weathered appearance that works especially well in cottage gardens, farmhouse settings, and outdoor spaces.

Old Zinc Long Oval Bowl

The Old Zinc Long Oval Bowl is ideal for planting several small plants side by side, making it perfect for herb displays, succulents, or seasonal arrangements. Its elongated shape fits nicely on windowsills, tables, or outdoor ledges, and the wooden handles make it easy to move and reposition.

With its antique zinc finish and rustic style, this planter adds a natural, vintage feel to both indoor and outdoor spaces.

Old Zinc Pot

The Old Zinc Pot is a classic round planter with a traditional metal finish that suits a wide range of plants. Its simple shape makes it ideal for small potted plants, herbs, or flowers, and it fits easily into shelves, windowsills, or garden arrangements.

The pot includes a rubber stopper in the base, allowing you to choose between drainage for outdoor use or a closed base for indoor use. This flexibility makes it a practical option for both indoor plant displays and outdoor planting.

products.title: Claire Pot 22 cm
products.body_html:
The Claire Pot is a modern take on the classic tapered flower pot, designed to suit both indoor and outdoor spaces. With its natural stone look and clean shape, it works beautifully in contemporary homes, patios, balconies, and gardens. The pot is lightweight yet durable, making it easy to move while still sturdy enough for long-term use.

Made from an eco-friendly blend of recycled plastic and natural stone powder, the Claire Pot is weather resistant and fully recyclable. It also features a built-in drainage system with a removable plug, allowing you to use it indoors without leaks or outdoors for proper drainage. This helps prevent overwatering and keeps plants healthier for longer.

Available in multiple colours and sizes, the Claire Pot is a versatile everyday planter that works with everything from small houseplants to larger outdoor arrangements.

products.title: Tasmanian tree fern | Dicksonia antarctica
products.body_html:
Tasmanian Tree Fern – Prehistoric, Spectacular, Garden Showpiece

Dicksonia antarctica is one of the most extraordinary plants you can grow in a UK garden — a living link to the Jurassic period that brings genuine, theatrical drama to any space it occupies. At 75 cm, this is a developing specimen with a thickening trunk and a full canopy of magnificent, arching, deep green fronds. It looks like it belongs in a lost world, and it will absolutely transform your garden.

Plant Personality & Aesthetic Appeal

The fronds of Dicksonia antarctica are large, deeply divided, and a rich, lustrous deep green — arching outward and downward from the crown in a wide, sweeping canopy that creates deep, dappled shade beneath. The fibrous, hairy trunk is covered in old frond bases and roots, giving it an ancient, textural quality that is completely unique. No two tree ferns look quite the same, and the sense of age and presence they carry is impossible to manufacture with any other plant. In a shaded garden, against a stone wall, or in a tropical-inspired planting scheme, Dicksonia is the undisputed centrepiece.

Growth Habit & Size Potential

Tree ferns grow very slowly — roughly 2.5 cm of trunk per year in UK conditions — so a specimen with any visible trunk has taken years to develop. At 75 cm with a developing trunk, this is a genuinely valuable and characterful plant. Indoors in a bright, sheltered conservatory or outdoors in a sheltered, shaded garden, it will continue to develop its magnificent canopy of fronds each season. Suitable for large containers, woodland garden schemes, and sheltered courtyard planting.

Light & Location Needs

Dappled shade or partial shade is ideal — bright, indirect light rather than full sun. Dicksonia antarctica grows naturally in cool, shaded Tasmanian rainforests and performs best in similar conditions in the UK: sheltered from strong winds, protected from direct midday sun, and in a position with reliably good atmospheric humidity. A sheltered courtyard, the shade side of a building, or a woodland garden planting scheme are all excellent spots. Protect from hard frost in colder areas, particularly the crown.

Care Level & Maintenance Tips

The key care requirement is moisture, particularly for the trunk. In dry periods, watering the trunk directly (not just the soil) helps maintain plant health and encourages new frond growth. Keep the soil consistently moist but not waterlogged. In winter, stuff the crown loosely with straw or bracken for frost protection in colder regions. Remove old, damaged fronds cleanly at the base. Feed with a dilute balanced liquid fertiliser through the growing season. Handle the trunk carefully — avoid damaging the fibrous root structure on the outside.

Dicksonia antarctica: A Garden Moment That Belongs to No Other Plant

Owning a Tasmanian tree fern is a particular kind of gardening experience. It connects you to deep time — to an era long before gardens existed — and brings a kind of primordial, green majesty to even a modest outdoor space. Beneath the arching fronds, everything becomes quieter and more shaded and more interesting. It is the plant that defines a garden as belonging to someone who really cares about what they grow.

Why You'll Love the Tasmanian Tree Fern

  • Truly Prehistoric Character: One of the oldest plant forms on Earth, growing unchanged for millions of years.
  • Unmatched Dramatic Impact: No other hardy garden plant creates quite this level of theatrical presence.
  • Rare and Collectable: A genuinely special plant that collectors and serious gardeners actively seek out.
  • Air Purifying: Contributes to a fresh, humid microclimate in indoor and enclosed outdoor spaces.
  • Suits Indoor and Outdoor Use: Thrives in sheltered gardens, courtyards, and bright conservatories.

Own a Piece of the Jurassic

Dicksonia antarctica is not just a plant — it is a statement about what kind of gardener you are. If you want something extraordinary, something that genuinely stops people in their tracks, this is it. Plant it in a sheltered, shaded spot and give it the moisture it deserves.

What You'll Receive

Size at dispatch: 75 cm in height, and comes in a 19 cm pot
Care level: Medium
Pet safety: Non-toxic to cats and dogs. We still recommend discouraging pets from eating any plant.
